metadata.description: 四次挥手: FIN→ACK→FIN→ACK. Full-duplex closure (requires 4 steps). TIME_WAIT waits for 2MSL. Python code example to illustrate the concept of a full-duplex closure in TCP: ```python import socket def simulate_tcp_close(): # Create a TCP/IP socket sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM) # Connect the socket to the port where the server is listening server_address = ('localhost', 10000) sock.connect(server_address) try: # Send data message = b"Hello, world" print(f"sending {message}") sock.sendall(message) # Wait for a response amount_received = 0 amount_expected = len(message) while amount_received < amount_expected: data = sock.recv(16) amount_received += len(data) print(f"received {data}") # Simulate sending FIN and receiving ACK sock.shutdown(socket.SHUT_WR) # Send FIN print("sent shutdown (FIN)") # Receive the final ACK while True: data = sock.recv(16) if not data: break print(f"received {data}") finally: # Simulate receiving FIN and sending ACK, then sending FIN again to close fully sock.close() print("socket closed") simulate_tcp_close() ``` This Python code simulates a TCP connection and demonstrates the full-duplex closure process, including the four-way handshake for closing the connection. Note that this is a simplified simulation and does not include actual network communication or the TIME_WAIT state.

data_pointers.title: 四次挥手
